name: Herman Dilled Monkey Bread
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 cups Flour (to 3 1/2 cups, depending on egg size used)
3 tablespoons Fresh Dill Weed, chopped (or any other chopped fresh herb of your choice)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the first three ingredients in a small bowl.
2. Stir until combined and set aside.
3. In a large mixing bowl, mix the next four ingredients (including yeast mixture).
4. Beat well and add 2 1/2 to 3 cups of flour by hand. Add more flour if the dough seems too soft for kneading.
5. Knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth. Use part of the flour measurement for the kneading process.
6. Grease a bowl with butter, turn the dough into it, and cover with floured plastic wrap.
7. Place in a warm, draft-free spot to rise until doubled.
8. Punch down the dough and cover; allow to rest for 10 minutes on a floured surface.
9. Roll the dough to a thickness of 1/2 to 2/3 inch.
10. Cut into uneven squares using a sharp knife or pizza cutter.
11. Dip each square in melted butter, then roll in chopped fresh herb until lightly coated according to taste.
12. Toss the coated squares into a greased monkey bread pot or solid bottom tube pan in an haphazard arrangement for a more interesting design.
13. Drizzle leftover melted butter over top and sprinkle with remaining herbs.
14. Cover and bake at 350°F for 35-40 minutes until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean and sounds hollow when tapped.
15. Serve warm, carefully transferring to a serving plate.
